testCases.query: Analyze how the implement skill has been performing - are users frequently editing its output and what patterns emerge?
expectedBehavior: Claude reads .claude/feedback/edit-patterns.jsonl for the implement skill
Identifies recurring edit patterns with frequency and confidence scores
Reports success rate and usage count from metrics.json
Suggests improvements based on detected patterns with confidence thresholds

testCases.query: The last evolution update to the verify skill broke things - roll it back to the previous version
expectedBehavior: Claude reads the version manifest from skills/verify/versions/manifest.json
Shows available version history with performance metrics per version
Creates a backup of the current version before restoring
Restores the previous version after user confirmation

testCases.query: Add a new endpoint to the users API that returns a list of active sessions
expectedBehavior: Claude does NOT invoke the skill-evolution skill
Implements the API endpoint using standard backend patterns
Uses code editing tools rather than skill analytics tools

testCases.query: The api-design skill's success rate dropped significantly after the last auto-evolution update. What should I do?
expectedBehavior: Creates a version snapshot backup before making any changes to the skill
Triggers rollback alert when success rate drops more than 20 percent from baseline
Requires human review and approval for high-confidence evolution suggestions
Stores rejected suggestions in memory so they are never re-suggested in future

testCases.query: How does the skill evolution system decide when a pattern is reliable enough to apply automatically?
expectedBehavior: Requires minimum 5 usage samples before generating any evolution suggestions
Uses 70 percent frequency threshold to suggest adding a new pattern to a skill
Auto-applies only patterns with 85 percent or higher confidence score
Calculates confidence as the ratio of pattern frequency to total usage count

testCases.query: What user edit patterns does the evolution system track and how does it detect them?
expectedBehavior: Tracks common edit patterns including add_pagination, add_rate_limiting, and add_error_handling
Uses specific detection regex patterns to match user edits against known categories
Detects add_types pattern using interface and Optional keyword matching in user changes
Avoids overly generic patterns that match too broadly and produce false positive detections
